name: Sierra de Ávila and Puerto de las Fuentes Loop
name_original: Sierra de Ávila y Puerto de las Fuentes — circular desde Ávila
description: This moderate 51.0-mile road cycling route from Ávila takes you through the scenic Sierra de Ávila, climbing to Las Fuentes Pass.
region_intro: Road cycling around Ávila offers diverse terrain in Spain's highest provincial capital, situated at over 3,710 feet (1,130 meters) above sea level. The region is characterized by challenging mountain passes within the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Ávila ranges, providing significant climbs and panoramic views. Cyclists can find varied routes on quiet country roads, traversing landscapes that include historic towns and natural features like the Adaja River. This area provides a natural playground for road cyclists seeking elevation profiles and scenic backdrops. Best road cycling routes around Ávila The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Ávila and Puerto de las Fuentes Loop, a 51.1 miles (82.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers picturesque scenery through the Sierra de Ávila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puertos de Arrebatacapas y Paramera - por el Embalse del Burguillo — circular desde Ávila, a difficult 54.5 miles (87.8 km) path. This route features significant ascents and requires very good fitness. Local road cyclists also love the Sancti Spiritu Bridge – Río Chico Park loop from Ávila, a 24.2 miles (38.9 km) trail leading through the area around the Adaja River, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Road cycling around Ávila is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Ávila, historic towns, and quiet country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to advanced road cycling challenges. The routes in Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Ávila?
A: Road cycling around Ávila offers a diverse and often challenging terrain. Situated at over 1,130 meters (3,710 ft) above sea level, the region is characterized by significant climbs and panoramic views, especially within the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierra_de_Gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; and Sierra de Ávila mountain ranges. You'll find routes on quiet country roads, traversing landscapes that include historic towns and natural features like the Adaja River. Expect varied elevation profiles, from moderate loops to demanding mountain passes.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Ávila?
A: Yes, Ávila is a haven for experienced road cyclists seeking challenging climbs. The region boasts notable passes such as the Three Passes of Gredos, Puerto de Peñanegra y Chía, and the Centenera Pass. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062297511/puertos-de-arrebatacapas-y-paramera-por-el-embalse-del-burguillo-circular-desde-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Arrebatacapas y Paramera - por el Embalse del Burguillo — circular desde Ávila&lt;/a&gt; feature significant ascents and require very good fitness, offering a true test of endurance.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ávila?
A: While Ávila is known for its challenging terrain, there are options for beginners and those looking for a more relaxed ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sancti Spiritu Bridge – Río Chico Park loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 24.2 miles (38.9 km) trail that leads through the area around the Adaja River, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. This route provides a gentler introduction to road cycling in the region.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling near Ávila?
A: Cycling routes around Ávila often provide views of or pass by significant historical landmarks. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila&lt;/a&gt; are a prominent feature, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;. Other historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5743721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Cogotas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach, offering a blend of physical activity and cultural immersion.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Ávila?
A: Absolutely. Given Ávila's high elevation and mountainous surroundings, many routes offer breathtaking panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Canchal&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Los_Cuatro_Postes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cuatro Postes&lt;/a&gt; monument just outside the city provides perhaps the most iconic views of Ávila's walled city, especially stunning at sunset.

Q: What natural features define the cycling landscape around Ávila?
A: The cycling landscape around Ávila is defined by its rugged natural beauty. Key features include the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierra_de_Gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gredos Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the Sierra de Ávila mountain ranges, which provide dramatic backdrops and challenging climbs. The Adaja River flows through the region, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the wide landscapes of the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Ávila?
A: The best season for road cycling in Ávila is generally spring and autumn. During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, offering pleasant temperatures for tackling the region's climbs and enjoying the scenic views. Summer can be quite hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter can bring cold temperatures and even snow in the higher mountain passes.

Q: Is road cycling in Ávila possible during winter?
A: Road cycling in Ávila during winter is possible, but it requires careful planning. While some lower-elevation routes might remain accessible, the higher mountain passes in the Sierra de Gredos and Sierra de Ávila can experience cold temperatures, ice, or snow, making them challenging or impassable. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts and route conditions before heading out during the colder months.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Ávila?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Ávila are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068159397/sierra-de-avila-and-puerto-de-las-fuentes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ávila and Puerto de las Fuentes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers picturesque scenery, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063814006/embalse-de-las-cogotas-mingorria-y-cardenosa-circular-desde-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Cogotas-Mingorría y Cardeñosa — circular desde Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, providing a moderate ride through the countryside.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Ávila?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Ávila, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to quiet country roads, and the stunning panoramic views. The combination of physical challenge and the rich historical backdrop, including views of the ancient city walls, is frequently highlighted as a unique and rewarding experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ávila?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Ávila on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy loops suitable for beginners to very difficult paths designed for seasoned cyclists seeking significant ascents and demanding challenges.

Mountain pass located in the center of the province of Ávila and which is 1,499 meters above sea level and connects the Amblés valley with San Juan del Olmo and the Muñico valley.

Small town that is located in what is called the Valle de Amblés and that is a few kilometers from its capital, Ávila. From the core, you can begin to see the first forms of the Sierra de Paramera and Gredos.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Ávila y Puerto de las Fuentes cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily a road cycling trail with mostly well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for road bikes. While it features ascents and descents characteristic of mountain passes, the overall difficulty is moderate, indicating manageable climbs without excessively steep or technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque scenery traversing the Sierra de Ávila, known for its natural beauty and mountainous landscapes. The route offers views of mountains, valleys, and forests, including pine, oak, and holm oak, along with fragrant Mediterranean shrubs like mastic trees, rockrose, and rosemary.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll pass by several points of interest. These include the Hermitage of Our Lady of Rihondo, the scenic Las Fuentes Pass, and the settlement of Muñogalindo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ávila region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions. While specific seasonal details are not provided, considering the 'moderate' difficulty and mountainous terrain, spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential cold/snow in winter at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Since this is a circular route starting and ending in Ávila, a historic city, you can find various parking options within Ávila itself to conveniently begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as 'moderate' and requires a good level of fitness due to its distance (approximately 82.2 km) and elevation gain (around 880 meters). While not excessively steep, it's more suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of several other paths, including the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, Ruta Teresiana, and Camino de Santiago del Sureste.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Ávila y Puerto de las Fuentes route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it is generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists, especially given the distance and potential traffic. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Sierra de Ávila y Puerto de las Fuentes route. It uses public roads and passes through natural areas accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's distance and moderate difficulty, you should pack essentials like sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a charged phone. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through the settlement of Muñogalindo, which may offer some services, it's always advisable to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your ride, as specific refreshment stops are not guaranteed throughout the entire 82.2 km loop.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Rihondo
